Container.Name: SelfAuthenticator
Container.Overview: Self-hosted, zero-knowledge 2FA / TOTP vault - your own alternative to Synology Secure SignIn or Authy. Stores TOTP secrets end-to-end encrypted (Argon2id + XChaCha20-Poly1305); the server only ever sees ciphertext. Single Docker container with SQLite - no external database. The web UI is an installable PWA and the same API will back the upcoming Android app.

Config.Name: Daten-Pfad
Config.Description: Path where the SQLite database (encrypted vault) is stored.

Config.Name: WebUI Port
Config.Description: Port for the SelfAuthenticator web interface and API.

Config.Name: Master Secret
Config.Description: REQUIRED. Long random secret (min. 32 chars). Signs JWTs and derives the anti-enumeration salt. It does NOT encrypt your vault (that happens client-side). Generate with: python -c "import secrets; print(secrets.token_hex(32))"

Config.Name: Allow Registration
Config.Description: Allow self-registration of new accounts. Set to false after creating your account to close the server.

Config.Name: DB Path
Config.Description: Path to SQLite database. Usually no need to change.
